comment: Shared contract for Japanese pitch. The popup renders pitch in JavaScript and note fields render it in Python; those are two implementations of one algorithm, and this fixture is the only thing holding them together. `morae` and `levels` exist on both sides and are asserted by BOTH tests/test_notes_markers.py and tests/js/scanner-core.test.js — a case added there must pass in both. `categories` is Python-only: the popup does not render categories, so there is no JavaScript counterpart to keep in step.

morae.name: plain kana are one mora each
small kana attach to the preceding mora
katakana small kana behave the same
a leading small kana has nothing to attach to
an empty reading has no morae

levels.name: heiban: low then high, staying high onto the particle
odaka: high to the end, drops on the particle
an explicit HL pattern is used verbatim
a short HL pattern repeats its last value
a long HL pattern is truncated
an unusable position is treated as heiban
no morae still reports the particle

categories.name: odaka is a drop on the particle, so position equals mora count
an explicit pattern has no plain-downstep category
